thread_tweets.text: some thoughts on the discourse here cover this
thread_tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
A lot of the trading discourse is poor because people overly focus on being right/wrong (batting avg) at a point in time (one at bat), rather than looking at slugging percent/wOBA (how much do you make when you're right vs lose when you're wrong)

Sizing+riding convictions >>

thread_tweets.text: @RJCcapital I’m all for simple trades, but they don’t always look simple in the beginning. Reminds me a lot of how in January 2024 when we were pitching Hynix and Micron on HBM content people said “why wouldn’t you just do the simple thing and buy more Nvidia?”.

thread_tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Our January review of our AI allocation made a pretty bold claim that $MU & SK Hynix would double within the next 2 years.

It’s been 6 months. So far, YTD:

Micron +72.6%
SK Hynix +57.9%

Our other HBM picks have also done quite well:

$ONTO +43.9%
Hanmi Semi +193% https://t.co/B4ECpEUwPK

tweets.text:
We’ve done less “In Conversation” articles recently as our audience expressed a preference for more thematic and analytical work. However, I really think there’s a lack of content that involves CEOs simply explaining what it is their company does, how they make money and what their long term vision is. 

Going back to read this conversation we had with the CEO of $OUST, it’s a really excellent resource for anyone who wants to get the story in their own words (as the stock has had a wildly volatile climb and quadrupled since we originally looked into it). 

Should we do some more of these sit downs with management?
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Shortly after adding $OUST to our robotics basket on May 16th, we had the pleasure of sitting down with CEO Angus Pacala to discuss all things LiDaR and Ouster’s path to profitability.

Since we first went long, OUST has risen a staggering 139%. 

https://t.co/oPZ0MCpBm8

thread_tweets.text: @Pentosh1 @MoneyPrinter0x some healthy pushback here; Elon still bottlenecked + you’d have to buy TSLA which is just one piece of a much larger co, far from a pure play
thread_tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
@MoneyPrinter0x [Claude]
QNX is the opt-in safety certification on the regulated IGX Thor path, while the mass-humanoid workhorse Jetson Thor defaults to Linux and ROS. The unit ramp is slower because the binding constraint is physical rather than software, namely actuators and China-gated

On Friday (repost), I noted the US announced it attacked Iran 36 minutes after the NYSE closed.

Well:

23 minutes after the Asian markets opened this evening,

The US and Iran have agreed to stop attacking each other before peace talks resume this week over the Strait of Hormuz and other issues, paving the way to end days of tit-for-tat attacks that tested a fragile truce.

https://t.co/smUPJ3xjGC
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Centcom posted this 36 minutes after the NYSE closed for the weekend.

Coincidence

tweets.text: Important read on time and life.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
In 2015 a writer named Tim Urban sat down and counted the days he had left with his parents. He was 34, healthy, both parents alive and well. The number came back around 300. Less time than he spent with them in any single year of his childhood.

The post is called The Tail End,
tweets.full_text: Important read on time and life.

the irony of china leading open source while america throttles the release of gpt 5.6 and fable
